stories.title: Trump slaps 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney vows to 'intensify' trade talks
stories.summary: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

stories.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
stories.summary: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

stories.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
stories.summary: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

stories.title: Row escalates in Ukraine over army chief's future
stories.summary: Protesters are demanding Oleksandr Syrskyi be removed and former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov reinstated by Friday.

stories.title: Dozens hurt in Bologna protests after man dies while being restrained by police
stories.summary: Thousands of people took to the streets demanding justice for a Moroccan-born man who died on Sunday.

stories.title: 'Anti-ICE' army veteran started fire outside Manhattan federal building, say officials
stories.summary: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, was armed with two axes, a machete, three knives and fireworks, says the FBI.

stories.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
stories.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

stories.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
stories.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

stories.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
stories.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

stories.summary: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

stories.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
stories.summary: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

stories.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
stories.summary: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

stories.title: Import prices post surprise gain as costs of goods from China hit highest since 2008
stories.summary: Import prices were up 0.3% for the month, as a drop in energy was more than offset by increases elsewhere.

stories.title: World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says
stories.summary: The latest edition of the Fed's Beige Book indicated that the soccer tournament wasn't necessarily translating to broader economic growth.

stories.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline
stories.summary: As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to the brief pause in tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

stories.title: Renewed Hormuz hostilities drive ECB rates rethink amid ‘extremely volatile’ outlook
stories.summary: Several consecutive days of strikes exchanged between the U.S. and Iran have cast uncertainty on the European Central Bank's interest rate decision next week.

stories.title: China posts slowest quarterly growth since 2022 as investment slumps, fanning stimulus calls
stories.summary: That second-quarter growth came below Beijing's full-year growth target range of 4.5% to 5%, the least ambitious goal in decades.

stories.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
stories.summary: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.

stories.title: DA: Cop covered bodycam to snap nude prisoners on his iPhone—but other cams caught him
stories.summary: Pennsylvania cop charged with oppression and obstruction.

stories.title: Firefighting drones in the works as wildfires plague US nearly year-round
stories.summary: California and XPRIZE competition tests whether drones can stop wildfires early.

stories.title: Judge halts Paramount's $111B purchase of Warner Bros. in win for US states
stories.summary: Judge grants restraining order, saying merger "likely to violate antitrust laws."

stories.title: The Space Force is now seeking to buy up to $30 billion in rocket launches
stories.summary: The Trump administration is asking the Space Force to do a lot. This will require more launches.

stories.title: AT&T loses key ruling in bid to stop offering basic phone service in California
stories.summary: AT&T suffers setback but will keep asking court and FCC to preempt state rules.

stories.title: RIP bargain bin: The price impact of Sony's disc-free PlayStation plan
stories.summary: Used discs are often cheaper than even deep digital discounts.

stories.title: CoinShares debuts Bitcoin mining ETF in Europe entrance
stories.summary: The UCITS ETF, CoinShares’ first in Europe, began trading on Deutsche Börse Xetra, tracking a rules-based index of publicly listed BTC miners.

stories.title: Robinhood-backed DEX Arcus expands with tokenized assets, perps
stories.summary: Arcus, built by the dYdX team, adds tokenized stocks and perpetual futures on Robinhood Chain as platforms compete to bring traditional assets onchain.

stories.title: Tether-backed Twenty One, Strike merger plan scrapped: Bloomberg
stories.summary: Strike will remain a standalone company after the proposed three-way merger was scrapped, while Twenty One Capital and Elektron continue discussions, Bloomberg reported.

stories.title: More MiCA-licensed crypto firms may exit EU market: Gate Europe CEO
stories.summary: Gate Europe’s Giovanni Cunti says some MiCA-licensed crypto firms may struggle to sustain compliance costs as Europe enters its new regulatory era.
